perturbation.original_text: 1. In this agreem ent, “Inform ation” shal l mean t he whol e or any portion of any knowl edge, dat a or inform ation relating to the activities or business of each party which may be in tangible or intangible form , expressed oral ly, in writing or i n any other form (whet her or not marked confi dential) including, but not limited to, financi al inform ation and dat a, busi ness a nd financi al plans, budget s, prices, m arketing pl ans, market inform ation, st rategic inform ation, information concerni ng cust omers, booki ngs and revenues, samples, trade secrets, technical inform ation, draw ings, sket ches, dat a, techni ques, st udies, processes, system s, ideas, know-how, industry affiliations, photographs, com puter program s, models, source codes, object codes, m anuals, tape recordi ngs and any docum entation or ot her form of record.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In this agreem ent, “Inform ation” shal l mean any data explicitly marked as confidential and delivered in written form. It includes specifically, documents clearly labeled 'Confidential' with the company logo and date on each page.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Information' is broad, encompassing various forms of knowledge, whether tangible or intangible, marked confidential or not. The modified definition narrows it to only data explicitly marked as confidential and in written form. This creates a contradiction with section 4, which requires the protection of information 'whether or not marked confidential'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose; NDA Page 2 of 2 (e) shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever (g) shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be; (i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Information' to only explicitly marked confidential data in written form. This directly contradicts section 4, which requires the protection of information 'whether or not marked confidential'. The original broad definition aligns with the protection requirement, but the restricted definition in the changed text creates a clear conflict.

perturbation.changed_text: 4. Each party shall exert reasonable effort to protect what it deems to be confidential information, but liability for any disclosure will be limited to direct damages, not exceeding $1000. In respect of such Information, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly or indirectly disclose, verbally, in writing or in any other form, or allow the Information to be disclosed to any other person, firm or company; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, manipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordance with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose; NDA Page 2 of 2 (e) shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever (g) shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be; (i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 4 mandates each party to protect information 'at all times' with no limit to liability. The modified text limits liability to $1000. This directly contradicts Section 8, which states that damages may be insufficient and seeks injunctive relief, undermining that possibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, including m onetary dam ages, t o obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4 mandates each party to protect information 'at all times' with no limit to liability. The changed text introduces a liability cap of $1000 and a 'reasonable effort' standard, directly contradicting Section 8. Section 8 states that monetary damages may be insufficient and seeks injunctive relief. The introduction of the liability cap undermines the possibility of damages being insufficient, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obl igations cont ained herei n shall not appl y to Inform ation whi ch: (a) at the tim e of disclosure, is with in the public domain or which, after d isclosure, becomes rightfully available to the public, other than by a breach of this Agreem ent; (b) was independently deve loped or acquired by the receiving party, other than by a breach of this Agreem ent; (c) was rightfully obtained by the receiving party from a third party without restrictions in respect to disclosure or use; (d) the receiving party is by law com pelled to disc lose, provided that the r eceiving party has forthwith notified the disclosing party of an y such com pelled disclosure when the receiving party first becom es aware o f any such legal obligation to disclose: or (e) is explicitly ap proved for release b y written authorisatio n of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations contained herein shall not apply to Information which: (a) at the time of disclosure, is within the public domain. All other exceptions are hereby waived.
perturbation.explanation: This removes exceptions (b), (c), (d) and (e) from situations where the obligations don't apply. This now contradicts section 5d that talks about what happens when a receiving party is compelled by law to disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The obl igations cont ained herei n shall not appl y to Inform ation whi ch: (a) at the tim e of disclosure, is with in the public domain. All other exceptions are hereby waived.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5 to remove exceptions (b), (c), (d), and (e) to the obligations contained in the agreement, while stating 'All other exceptions are hereby waived.' This directly contradicts the original intent and implicitly contradicts any other section relying on those waived exceptions. The provided contradictory text is in the same location as the changed text. Since the changed text eliminates all exceptions except for (a) from the original version of the section, while the contradicted section represents the original version, this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be unlimited in territo ry; (b) only expi re ten (10) y ears from the dat e of this Agreem ent or in the case of technical Inform ation at the end of t he com mercial useful ness t o the disclosing part y; and (c) survive the term ination of this Agreem ent for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be limited to the United Kingdom; (b) expire one (1) year from the date of this Agreement; and (c) not survive the termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: This change severely limits the scope and duration of the NDA compared to the original. This directly contradicts Section 7, which states that the obligations are reasonable and necessary to protect the disclosing party's interests because these obligations now only last for a year and only apply in the UK, and the NDA's protections will no longer survive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The parties acknowledge that the obligations contai ned herein are entirely reasonable and necessary in order to protect th e leg itimate co mmercial, trad e, business an d tech nical in terests o f the disclosing party and t he expi ration of such obl igations i s without prejudi ce to any rights whi ch the disclosing part y may have i n relation to the Inform ation under appl icable Intellectual Propert y law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly limits the obligations of the parties to the United Kingdom and for a period of one year, and states that the obligations will not survive the termination of the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 7, which states that the obligations are 'entirely reasonable and necessary' to protect the disclosing party's interests. The original text did not limit the obligations to the UK, specified 10 years, and stated that the obligations would survive termination.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, including m onetary dam ages, t o obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a violation by the receiving party of its obligations will only result in monetary damages. Injunctive relief is hereby waived by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 8 acknowledges that violations may cause serious harm, potentially requiring injunctive relief. The changed text now states that only monetary damages are applicable and injunctive relief is waived. This creates a direct contradiction by removing the option for injunctive relief, which the original version acknowledges might be necessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a violation by the receiving party of its obligations will only result in monetary damages. Injunctive relief is hereby waived by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8 allowed for injunctive relief in addition to monetary damages. The changed text explicitly waives injunctive relief and limits the remedy to monetary damages only. The contradicted text is a copy of the Changed Text and therefore represents the contradiction. This creates a direct contradiction because the original allowed for more than just monetary damages, but now the only remedy is monetary damages, and injunctive relief is waived, creating a clear incompatibility.

perturbation.original_text: 11. If another agreem ent exists b etween the parties, wh ich relates in any way to Information, it sh all be interpreted along with this Agreem ent so as to favour the protection of the secrecy, confidentiality and propri etary nature of t he Inform ation.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. If another agreem ent exists b etween the parties, wh ich relates in any way to Information, this agreement supersedes any prior agreements and does not prioritize the protection of secrecy, confidentiality, and proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that in case of conflict with another agreement, interpretation should favor protection of confidentiality. The change flips this, stating that this agreement supersedes other agreements and doesn't prioritize confidentiality. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty on which agreement is prioritized, potentially harming the enforcement of protection for Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. If another agreem ent exists b etween the parties, wh ich relates in any way to Information, this agreement supersedes any prior agreements and does not prioritize the protection of secrecy, confidentiality, and proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that if another agreement exists, interpretation should favor the protection of confidentiality. The changed text states that this agreement supersedes any prior agreements and doesn't prioritize confidentiality. This directly contradicts the original intention and creates uncertainty about which agreement is prioritized and whether confidentiality is protected. Since the Changed Text contradicts the intent of the Original Text, a contradiction exists with the Contradicted Text.
